source.title: Striatum and pre-SMA facilitate decision-making under time pressure
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA

confidence_rationale: Good mapping to the threshold-gating half of ARC-016's circuit: striatum and pre-SMA activation correlates with response threshold adjustment under speed pressure, directly instantiating the claim that a BG-frontal circuit modulates action-selection gating based on a threshold parameter. Individual variation in striatum activation predicts individual variation in threshold — confirming the threshold is a circuit-level variable, not just behavioural. Moderate transfer risk from response-speed pressure to REE's precision-derived commitment threshold.
failure_signatures: The threshold manipulation is externally imposed (speed-accuracy cues) not internally derived from prediction uncertainty, unlike ARC-016's E3-derived variance mechanism.
Pre-SMA/striatum are motor circuit nodes; ARC-016's BetaGate is conceptually at the cognitive control level, requiring an additional inference that the same circuit logic generalises upward.
fMRI temporal resolution cannot resolve whether threshold is set before or during the deliberation phase, which matters for the E3-derived variance → threshold → gate ordering ARC-016 requires.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Under speed pressure, striatum and pre-SMA are activated; individual variation in these activations correlates with individual variation in response threshold (lower threshold = faster/less accurate). The basal ganglia-frontal circuit implements threshold modulation for action gating.
mapping.ree_translation: The striatum-pre-SMA threshold modulation circuit is the neural analog of ARC-016's BetaGate: the BG-frontal circuit sets a commitment threshold and gates when accumulated evidence (or in REE, when E3-derived variance crosses a relative threshold) permits action release. The REE BetaGate's commit-rate vs precision relationship (EXQ-018b: 40% precision drop → 40% commit-rate drop) directly parallels the threshold-activation correlation this study documents.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Forstmann et al. vary threshold via externally imposed speed-accuracy instructions; ARC-016 requires threshold to emerge from E3's internal uncertainty estimate. The neural circuit is the same but the driving signal differs. The inference is that the same BG-frontal gating circuit that implements instruction-driven threshold adjustment also implements E3-driven threshold adjustment.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI, n=19, two-alternative forced choice perceptual decision task with speed-accuracy emphasis cues, drift-diffusion model parameter extraction

confidence_components.notes: PNAS journal, human data, strong individual-differences correlation design. Transfer risk from externally-driven to internally-derived threshold. Mapping fidelity penalised for this distinction.
